column2: Noville, Belgium, December 19th, 1944. American tanks and paratroopers hold Noville against the full weight of German armor. Board rules: all half hexes on the North edge of boards 6 and 9 are woods, except 6A5 and 9A5. FOG RULE: -1 to Infantry FP per 4 hexes of range (infantry not considered moving in open)
column3: vehicles must take a Prof Check firing with -1 Prof per 4 hexes
column4: SATW Checks get -1 Morale. Setup order: all German Infantry first, then all Americans, then all German Vehicles.

column2: The German player wins by having at least 3 squads in buildings on board 1 and/or board 3 at the end of game turn 8.

Boards 1 and 3: 2 Squads, 1 Paratrooper w/ Bazooka, 5 DECOY, 2 M4A3(76), 3 M18 Tank Destroyers. Boards 6 and 9, hex rows E and F: 10 Squads, 3 Mortars, 2 MG, 2 DECOY, 17 Foxholes. Units on boards 6 and 9 may NOT voluntarily move on Turn 1 (they may fire).
The American player may exchange 1 Paratrooper MG to add 1 Paratrooper w/ Bazooka to that group.
Boards 6 and 9, hex rows A, B, and C: 18 1st Line, 3 MG WT. Boards 6 and 9, hex rows G and H: 4 PzVG, 4 PzIVH. Tanks may not set up adjacent to another tank.
